Title: Path-traversal prefix-match bypass in create_project()
Description: The path-traversal guard in src/launcher/core/project_creator.py uses str(target).startswith(str(destination.resolve())). A folder_name whose resolved path shares the destination string prefix but is not inside the destination (e.g. destination='/tmp/foo', folder_name='../foobar' -> target='/tmp/foobar') passes the check and allows shutil.copytree() to write outside the authorised directory.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Call create_project(template, dest, '../foobar') where dest ends in 'foo'. 2. Observe: no ValueError raised; directory created at sibling path. 3. See test_project_creator_rejects_prefix_match_bypass.
Actual Behaviour: shutil.copytree() executes successfully; template copied to sibling directory outside destination.
Comments: Fix: replace str(target).startswith(str(destination.resolve())) with target.is_relative_to(destination.resolve()). Developer claimed fix applied in Iteration 2 but source still contains the old guard. Tester Agent re-confirmed 2026-03-10 Iteration 2 re-review.

Title: security_gate.py missing from repository
Description: The primary deliverable for SAF-001 (security_gate.py) was compiled locally but never committed. Only the pyc cache exists at Default-Project/.github/hooks/scripts/__pycache__/security_gate.cpython-311.pyc. The source file is absent from the working tree and git history.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Clone/check working tree. 2. Look for Default-Project/.github/hooks/scripts/security_gate.py. 3. File not found.
Expected Behaviour: security_gate.py present in repository and tracked by git.
Actual Behaviour: File is absent; only a compiled .pyc cache exists. VS Code hook cannot function and code cannot be reviewed or tested.

Title: test_saf001_security_gate.py missing from repository
Description: The SAF-001 test file was compiled locally but never committed. Only tests/__pycache__/test_saf001_security_gate.cpython-311-pytest-9.0.2.pyc exists. The 49 test results logged as TST-033 to TST-081 in test-results.csv are unverifiable and unrunnable.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Run: python -m pytest tests/ -v. 2. Observe: 32 tests collected (INS-001 + INS-002 only). No SAF-001 tests collected.
Expected Behaviour: test_saf001_security_gate.py present in tests/ and all 49 tests collected and passing.
Actual Behaviour: Source file absent; pytest collects 0 SAF-001 tests.
Comments: Discovered during Tester review on 2026-03-10 (Iteration 1). TST-033 to TST-081 must be re-verified after source is restored.

Title: SAF-004 design doc — Stage 4 formal spec omits command chain separator handling
Description: Section 5 Stage 4 specification only says 'Split on whitespace respecting single and double quotes.' The requirement to split on ;, &&, and || and run Stage 5 on each resulting segment is only stated in Section 9.6 as a buried implementation requirement. An implementer following the normative Section 5 spec would build a tokenizer that misses chained commands like 'git status; rm -rf .'
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Read Section 5 Stage 4 spec only. 2. Implement tokenizer per that spec. 3. Run test T-043 (git status; rm -rf . → deny). 4. Test fails because ; is not treated as a command separator.
Expected Behaviour: Section 5 Stage 4 spec explicitly states that ;, && and || are treated as command chain separators and each segment is analyzed independently.
Actual Behaviour: Stage 4 spec is silent on chain separators; the requirement only appears in Section 9.6 edge cases.
Comments: Discovered during Tester review on 2026-03-10. Fix: add chain-separator rule to Section 5 Stage 4 normative spec.

Title: SAF-004 design doc — escape hatch residual checks (Section 12.4) don't cover full pre-scan catalogue
Description: Section 12.4 lists residual safety checks for exception-listed commands as: (1) No interpreter-chaining flags P-01 to P-09, (2) No encoded commands P-10, (3) Zone check on paths. Patterns P-11 to P-28 (IEX, Invoke-Expression, Start-Process, & $var, [Convert]::FromBase64String, pipe-to-interpreter, backtick subshell, $() subshell, eval, exec, source, dot-source, execution-policy bypass, Invoke-Item, Set-Alias, New-Alias, process substitution) are not blocked by residual checks. A broad exception pattern could allow these vectors.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Create an exception entry matching 'curl https://api.example.com'. 2. Submit command: 'curl https://api.example.com; eval $payload'. 3. Command matches exception (if pattern allows semicolons), residual check passes (eval is not in P-01 to P-10), zone check passes. Command would reach 'ask' instead of 'deny'.
Expected Behaviour: All pre-scan patterns P-01 to P-28 are applied as residual checks; no exception can override any pattern in _OBFUSCATION_PATTERNS.
Actual Behaviour: Only P-01 to P-10 are in residual checks; P-11 to P-28 can be bypassed via exception entry.
Comments: Discovered during Tester review on 2026-03-10. Fix: replace enumeration in Section 12.4 with 'apply full _OBFUSCATION_PATTERNS list to exception-matched commands.'

Title: SAF-004 design doc — P-10 implementation reference code not updated with Section 9.4 fix
Description: Section 9.4 correctly identifies that P-10 (re.compile(r'(?:powershell|pwsh)[^\n]*?-enc(?:odedcommand)?\b')) does not match the -e short flag abbreviation of -EncodedCommand. The prose provides a corrected pattern. However the implementation reference code block at the end of Section 6 still shows the original incomplete pattern. An implementer using Section 6 as the authoritative source would implement the vulnerable version.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Copy _OBFUSCATION_PATTERNS code block from Section 6. 2. Run test T-050 (powershell -e BASE64 → deny). 3. Test fails because the P-10 pattern does not match -e.
Expected Behaviour: Section 6 code block contains the corrected P-10 pattern from Section 9.4.
Actual Behaviour: Section 6 code block still has the original P-10 pattern lacking -e coverage.
Comments: Discovered during Tester review on 2026-03-10. Fix: update Section 6 code block with corrected P-10 pattern.

Title: Duplicate TST IDs in test-results.csv
Description: TST-125 through TST-165 were originally assigned to SAF-002 tests. GUI-001 Developer then reused TST-125 through TST-158 for GUI-001 tests. INS-012 Developer additionally reused TST-125 through TST-147 for INS-012 tests. The CSV now contains three conflicting sets of entries sharing the same IDs.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Open docs/test-results/test-results.csv. 2. Search for TST-125. 3. Observe three different test names with the same ID.
Expected Behaviour: Each TST ID should be unique across the entire test-results.csv.
Actual Behaviour: Three separate sets of tests share IDs TST-125 to TST-147 (SAF-002 / GUI-001 / INS-012). Confuses traceability and audit.
Comments: Discovered during Tester review of INS-012 on 2026-03-11. Requires a CSV cleanup pass to renumber GUI-001 tests to TST-166+ and INS-012 tests to follow.

Title: SAF-004 design doc — pre-scan pattern count in Section 5 is wrong
Description: Section 5 Stage 3 block states 'Apply 15 regex patterns (Section 6).' The actual specification defines 23 patterns (P-01 to P-23) in Section 6 plus 5 more (P-24 to P-28) in Section 10 = 28 patterns total. The stale count '15' conflicts with the authoritative pattern list.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Read Section 5. 2. Count patterns listed in Section 6 code block: 23. 3. Note Section 5 says 15. 4. Note 5 additional patterns in Section 10. 5. Actual total: 28.
Expected Behaviour: Section 5 accurately states the total number of pre-scan patterns (28).
Actual Behaviour: Section 5 says 15 patterns; actual number is 28.
Comments: Discovered during Tester review on 2026-03-10. Fix: update Section 5 to reference 28 patterns or consolidate all patterns into Section 6 and remove split definitions.

Title: Non-null C0 control characters in path segments bypass deny zone
Description: normalize_path() in zone_classifier.py strips null bytes (\x00) but does not strip other ASCII C0 control characters (tab \x09, newline \x0a, CR \x0d, etc.). A path like /workspace/\t.github/secret normalises to /workspace/\t.github/secret; Method 1 gets first segment \t.github (not in _DENY_DIRS) and Method 2 pattern /(\.github) does not match /\t.github. classify() returns ask instead of deny.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Call zc.classify(WS + chr(9) + .github/secret, WS). 2. Observe result == ask. 3. Expected result == deny. (See test_security_tab_before_deny_dir TST-184 and test_security_newline_before_deny_dir TST-185)
Expected Behaviour: classify() returns deny for any path where a deny-zone directory name is preceded only by C0 control characters.
Actual Behaviour: classify() returns ask, allowing prompt-injection bypass of the deny gate.
Comments: Fixed in SAF-002 Iteration 2: normalize_path() now uses re.sub(r'[\x00-\x1f]', '', p) to strip the full C0 range. TST-184 TST-185 TST-196 TST-197 TST-198 all pass.

Title: UNC path to project/ outside workspace incorrectly returns allow
Description: Method 2 _ALLOW_PATTERN = re.compile(r/project(/|$)) matches any /project/ substring in the normalised path. For a UNC path like \\\\server\\share\\project\\file.py normalised to /server/share/project/file.py, Method 1 raises ValueError (not under ws_root) and falls through to Method 2 which finds /project/ and returns allow. An agent accessing a network share at a path containing the word project receives allow instead of ask.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Call zc.classify(\\\\\\\\server\\\\share\\\\project\\\\sensitive.py, /workspace). 2. Observe result == allow. 3. Expected result == ask. (See test_security_unc_path_project_outside_workspace_not_allow TST-186)
Expected Behaviour: classify() returns ask for any allow pattern match where the normalised path does not start with ws_root.
Actual Behaviour: classify() returns allow, bypassing the human-approval requirement for out-of-workspace access.
Comments: Fixed in SAF-002 Iteration 2: Method 2 allow branch now guards with norm.startswith(ws_clean + '/') or norm == ws_clean. TST-186 TST-199 TST-200 all pass.

Title: GUI-001 tests regressed — 38 of 41 tests fail
Description: 38 of 41 tests in tests/GUI-001/test_gui001_main_window.py fail when pytest collects the full test suite. Failures indicate the App class no longer exposes _window, _browse_destination, _on_create_project attributes and launcher.gui.components no longer has make_label_entry_row or make_browse_row functions. These attributes were required by the Tester-verified GUI-001 test suite (TST-175–TST-183, 207/207 pass on 2026-03-11).
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Run .venv\Scripts\python -m pytest tests/ -v. 2. Observe 38 failures all in tests/GUI-001/. 3. Failure types: AttributeError on _window, _browse_destination, _on_create_project; AttributeError on make_label_entry_row and make_browse_row in components; mock call-count assertions failing.
Expected Behaviour: All 41 GUI-001 tests pass (207/207 regression suite passed on 2026-03-11 per TST-183).
Actual Behaviour: 38 of 41 GUI-001 tests fail; src/launcher/gui/app.py and src/launcher/gui/components.py do not match the API expected by the tests. The GUI implementation has been modified without updating the tests (or vice versa).
Comments: Discovered during SAF-002 Iteration 2 Tester review on 2026-03-11. SAF-002 changes are confined to Default-Project/.github/hooks/scripts/zone_classifier.py and are not the cause. This regression must be investigated and resolved in a subsequent WP. The 3 passing GUI-001 tests (test_app_class_is_importable
column13: test_headless_guard_skips_app_when_pytest_running) indicate the App class exists and is importable.
